summary: The Docker-based reproduction deploys a full YesWiki instance with MariaDB, sends a crafted JSONP request to the ajaxedit handler with an attacker-controlled callback parameter, and confirms that the callback value is reflected verbatim in the HTTP response body without sanitization.
expected_security_impact: The server response to /PagePrincipale/ajaxedit?jsonp_callback=<payload> should contain the attacker-controlled payload reflected verbatim in the response body, demonstrating that an attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript that auto-executes in victim browsers via JSONP script tags.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh deployed a full YesWiki environment (PHP 7.4, Apache, MariaDB), sent a curl request with the proof string EVOHUNT_JSONP_XSS_PROOF_7b2c as the jsonp_callback parameter to the ajaxedit handler, and the response body contained the proof string verbatim in the JSONP response wrapper.
verification_method: The verify.sh helper deploys the YesWiki environment, sends a crafted HTTP request with a unique proof string (EVOHUNT_JSONP_XSS_PROOF_7b2c) as the jsonp_callback parameter value, then uses grep to confirm the exact proof string appears in the response body. This distinguishes successful XSS reflection from generic server errors, redirects, or unrelated output.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP response body contains the literal string EVOHUNT_JSONP_XSS_PROOF_7b2c injected via the jsonp_callback parameter.
The response is served with Content-Type application/json, but the JSONP pattern allows loading via <script src> tags which execute the content as JavaScript.
The reflection occurs at ajaxedit.php line 93-94 which is outside any access-control check, confirming the vulnerability is exploitable without authentication.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction requires internet access during Docker build for composer dependency installation.
The setup takes several minutes due to MariaDB initialization and composer install.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the doryphore ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
